old_claim.note: Military Field Medicine is contextual infrastructure or shared knowledge, not a strict hard prerequisite.

new_claim_absent: No direct military field medicine dependency remains because the node is scoped to learned medical schools and faculties, not battlefield-care organization.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract: the review describes the Schola Medica Salernitana as a forerunner of modern university medical schools and discusses medical-school tradition.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports medieval medical-school lineage, not military field medicine as a direct prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Field medicine is a clinical-care setting; retaining it direct would confuse institutional learned medicine with battlefield logistics.

ontology_before: University Medical Faculties depended on Military Field Medicine as a weak common-dependency edge.
ontology_after: University Medical Faculties keeps herbal medicine preparation as remaining context while military field medicine is outside the direct prerequisite set.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: military_field_medicine is no longer a direct prerequisite for university_medical_faculties.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to military medical education rather than university medical faculties.
A stronger source showed Salerno-style medical faculties directly required military field medicine.
A separate military_medical_training node were added and scoped to field medicine.
short_reason: Field medicine is care context, not medical-faculty lineage.
